description: What holds for everything holds for anything in particular.
goal: (-> (forall x (-> (App P x) (App Q x))) (-> (App P a) (App Q a)))

hints.text: The goal says: if every $P$ is also a $Q$, then whenever something is $P$, it's also $Q$. This is just two nested [implication|implications] with a universal inside. Start by assuming the outermost antecedent: $\forall x.\; P(x) \to Q(x)$.
Assume the universal statement: everything P is Q.
Now you're proving $P(a) \to Q(a)$ under that [assumption|assumption]. Assume $P(a)$.
Instantiate the universal with the specific term $a$ using `forall-elim`. This gives you $P(a) \to Q(a)$.
[assumption|Discharge] the universal [premise|premise] to finish the full [implication|implication].
